title: Operation Eclipse — The Grand Capstone
prerequisites: Advanced Offensive Track - Mastery Tier Access
learning_objectives: Strategic operation planning and threat modeling
Comprehensive reconnaissance and OSINT across attack surfaces
Internal network campaign and domain dominance
Cloud environment assessment from domain-level access
Professional red team report development (40+ pages)
scenario: This Mastery mission requires professional-grade execution in Pan-African enterprise undergoing advanced red team assessment. Challenge: Execute complete red team operation spanning 30 days: reconnaissance through cloud, OT, mobile, and objective achievement. Relevance: The defining mission of OCH Mastery tier; demonstrates mastery across all offensive domains

scenario_context.setting: Pan-African enterprise undergoing advanced red team assessment
scenario_context.challenge: Execute complete red team operation spanning 30 days: reconnaissance through cloud, OT, mobile, and objective achievement
scenario_context.relevance: The defining mission of OCH Mastery tier; demonstrates mastery across all offensive domains

subtasks.title: Threat Intel & Op Plan
subtasks.description: Threat model, adversary profile, operational plan, ROE, infrastructure.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
For this subtask in the context of Operation Eclipse — The Grand Capstone:

    1. Understand the requirement clearly and document your approach
    2. Execute the task methodically with evidence collection
    3. Document findings with sufficient detail for peer review
    4. Consider African-specific contexts and implications
    5. Ensure all deliverables meet professional standards
    6. Prepare materials for senior mentor evaluation
subtasks.evidence_required: Op plan + ROE + infra design (20+ pages)

resources.title: Mission Overview: Operation Eclipse — The Grand Capstone

reflection_prompt: How did you balance speed versus stealth across your 30-day campaign? What would you do differently if operating against a nation-state level adversary?

quiz.question: In the 30-day Operation Eclipse capstone, what distinguishes strategic operation planning from standard red team assessments?
options: Strategic planning requires threat modeling, adversary profiling, multi-domain attack planning, and executive-level objectives—transforming the assessment into a simulated nation-state operation
Standard assessments are more thorough than strategic operations
Operation planning eliminates the need for technical execution
quiz.explanation: The Operation Eclipse capstone simulates advanced nation-state red team operations. This requires strategic planning: understanding the target organization deeply, developing threat profiles, planning multi-domain attack sequences, and defining executive-level objectives. This transforms a standard assessment into a sophisticated operation.

quiz.question: What is the relationship between technical execution in the internal domain campaign and strategic objectives in Operation Eclipse?
options: They are unrelated; technical execution happens independently of objectives
Technical execution directly enables strategic objectives; each pivot and compromise must advance toward executive-level goals (accessing source code, financial data, etc.)
Strategic objectives are irrelevant to technical work
Objectives are determined only after technical execution is complete
quiz.explanation: In Operation Eclipse, every technical action—initial access, lateral movement, privilege escalation—must advance toward predefined strategic objectives. This discipline transforms technical skill into strategic impact, demonstrating mastery-level red team capabilities.

portfolio_artifacts: Evidence of technical excellence and strategic thinking
mentor_notes:
This Mastery mission requires senior mentor review. Candidates must demonstrate:
- Professional-grade execution and documentation
- Deep understanding of African organizational and threat contexts
- Strategic thinking beyond tactical execution
- Readiness for commercial red team operations
- Leadership potential within OCH and broader community

Successful completion positions candidate for Mastery portfolio and professional practice.
